description: Authorized Copilot Chat model labels for APEX agents. Two-part design: `models` is hand-maintained metadata that authorizes which labels may appear in agent frontmatter; `assignments` is auto-generated from frontmatter (the canonical source) so the catalog can never drift from the agents themselves. The agent registry mirrors frontmatter via validate-models.mjs --only=consistency; the catalog enforces the label allow-list and assignment integrity via validate-models.mjs --only=catalog.

Claude Opus 4.8.notes: Anthropic's most capable model. Drop-in upgrade from Opus 4.7 (no API-breaking changes): same tools/features, adaptive thinking only, sampling params unsupported. Improves long-horizon agentic coding, reasoning-effort calibration, tool triggering, and compaction recovery; effort defaults to `high` on all surfaces. Authorized 2026-05 for Architecture (03) and IaC Planning (05). Reasoning-effort policy (high vs default) is a per-agent / per-call concern, NOT a separate model label — see `.github/instructions/agent-authoring.instructions.md`.

Claude Opus 4.7.notes: Active Opus 4.7 SKU. Reasoning-effort policy (high vs default) is a per-agent / per-call concern, NOT a separate model label. See `.github/instructions/agent-authoring.instructions.md` ("Reasoning-effort policy" section) for guidance on which agents run at high effort.

Claude Opus 4.6.notes: Deprecated 2026-05 in favor of Claude Opus 4.7. Retained for audit history; not authorized for new agents.

Claude Sonnet 5.notes: API model id `claude-sonnet-5`. Migrated 2026-06 from Claude Sonnet 4.6 (deprecated below) — drop-in upgrade for Step 3 Design, Step 5 IaC code generation (Bicep + Terraform), Step 7 As-Built documentation, Context Optimizer, and the IaC validation/preview subagents (bicep-validate, bicep-whatif, terraform-validate, terraform-plan, policy-precheck). The Anthropic prompting style (XML-tagged blocks, role-first, quote-grounded review, checklist-driven structured output) suits ADR + diagram authoring, the structured PASS/FAIL findings produced by the validation subagents, and the checklist-driven artifact generation of as-built docs. Key behavioral deltas from Sonnet 4.6: adaptive thinking is ON by default (manual extended thinking removed, returns 400); `effort` still defaults to `high` — kept at `high` for CodeGen (no `xhigh` escalation needed, AVM generation is structured execution, not deep reasoning); the new tokenizer produces ~30% more tokens for the same text (re-check `max_tokens` headroom and context budgets); the model follows instructions more literally — state scope explicitly; review/validation prompts should ask for full-coverage findings rather than relying on the model to self-filter, since Sonnet 5 follows conservative framing ('only report high-severity') more strictly than 4.6 did. Sampling params (`temperature`/`top_p`/`top_k`) remain unsupported, same as 4.6. Other GPT-5.5 cohort members (Governance, Deploy, Challenger, E2E, Diagnose) remain on GPT-5.5.

Claude Sonnet 4.6.notes: Deprecated 2026-06 in favor of Claude Sonnet 5. Retained for audit history; not authorized for new agents.

GPT-5.4.notes: Active OpenAI standard-tier SKU. Re-authorized 2026-05 after the deprecation flag was lifted (no `GPT-5.5 mini` exists, so the `GPT-5.4` cohort remains the lower-cost OpenAI tier alongside `GPT-5.5`). Pair with `GPT-5.4 mini` for utility/CLI prompts.

GPT-5.4 mini.notes: Lightweight, cost-optimized model for utility prompts and CLI-only workflows. Suitable for straightforward git operations, file staging, and deterministic CLI parsing. Not for complex orchestration or reasoning-heavy tasks.

GPT-5.5.notes: Outcome-first prompting per the OpenAI GPT-5.5 prompting guide: explicit Role / Personality / Goal / Success / Constraints / Output / Stop sections, retrieval budgets, decision rules over absolutes, and stopping conditions. More efficient reasoning than predecessors — re-evaluate low/medium effort before escalating to high. After 2026-05 cohort migrations, GPT-5.5 covers deploy (Bicep + Terraform), diagnostics, the e2e-orchestrator, and challenger agents. IaC code generation (06b/06t) moved to Claude Sonnet 4.6 (now Claude Sonnet 5) for AVM verbatim invariants; As-Built (08) moved to Claude Sonnet 4.6 (now Claude Sonnet 5) for XML-structured artifact authoring alignment.

GPT-5.3-Codex.notes: Code/maths-focused. Used by cost-estimate-subagent for numerical reasoning.

MAI-Code-1-Flash.notes: Microsoft's fast, cost-optimized coding model. Authorized 2026-06 for the Orchestrator (01), which performs handoff-only routing with no creative generation — a lightweight standard-tier model is sufficient. No MAI-specific automated prompting rules exist yet; the `mai-code` family is `reviewer-only` in vendor-prompting (see `.github/skills/vendor-prompting/references/family-support.md`). The orchestrator body retains its outcome-first skeleton (Role/Goal/Success/Constraints/Output/Stop), which remains a sound structure for a routing agent.

governance.source_of_truth: Agent frontmatter is canonical. The agent registry mirrors frontmatter (enforced by validate-models.mjs --only=consistency). The catalog `models` block authorizes which labels are allowed; the catalog `assignments` block is generated from frontmatter and must match it exactly (enforced by validate-models.mjs --only=catalog).
rules: Every model label appearing in agent or subagent frontmatter MUST exist as a key in `models`.
Every model label appearing in the agent registry MUST exist as a key in `models`.
The `assignments` block MUST match the output of `node tools/scripts/generate-model-catalog.mjs`. A lefthook pre-commit hook regenerates it whenever an agent frontmatter file is staged.
A model with `deprecated: true` MAY remain in `models` for audit history but MUST NOT appear in any active assignment (frontmatter or registry).

assignments.description: Auto-generated inventory of agent → model assignments derived from frontmatter (canonical source). Do not edit by hand; run `node tools/scripts/generate-model-catalog.mjs` or let the lefthook pre-commit hook refresh it when frontmatter changes.
